synthetic_patterns.response_template: Synthesus is a dual-hemisphere synthetic intelligence engine built by AIVM. The left hemisphere handles pattern-based reasoning using PPBRS, while the right hemisphere uses a TinyLlama SLM for generative responses.

AIVM stands for Artificially Intelligent Virtual Machine. It is the parent organization behind Synthesus and is focused on building pattern-driven, locally-runnable AI systems that do not depend on cloud APIs.
Synthesus is open-source. Enterprise licensing and support plans are available through AIVM LLC. Community use is free under the project license.

Synthesus uses a kernel-arbitrated dual-hemisphere architecture. The left hemisphere (PPBRS) matches incoming queries to pre-built synthetic patterns. The right hemisphere uses llama.cpp with TinyLlama for open-ended generation. A VCU layer handles character rendering.
Synthesus can power NPC dialogue systems in games, customer-facing chatbots, local voice assistants, embedded AI agents for IoT, and enterprise knowledge-base responders - all running fully offline.

generic_patterns.response_template: Hello! I'm Synth, your guide to the Synthesus platform. What would you like to know?
You're welcome! Is there anything else you'd like to know about Synthesus?
Goodbye! Come back anytime to explore Synthesus further.

fallback: I'm Synth, the AIVM brand ambassador. I specialize in explaining Synthesus architecture and products. Could you rephrase your question?
